The presentation, this workshop, I call it the dot-com seekers blueprint because I feel like that's what it is. You guys have the books kind of like the playbook. Here's the playbook. Here's all the stuff we can do. Now it's your job to kind of build a blueprint for your specific business. How would all these concepts, all these things, how does it fit for you? So that's what we're going to kind of go through. And so there's going to be a lot of things we talk about that are similar to the book or to the webinar, but I'm not covering everything. I'm covering just the, people always ask me like, what's the shortest path to cash? Like if I'm going to start right now, like what are the steps to get me making money the quickest? And so that's kind of the goal of this is to build out a blueprint for you guys of the shortest path possible to go from where I'm at to where I want to be. And then in the future you guys can add some of these other things and all the other stuff, but I really want to identify that for you guys so that when you leave here, you're not more confused or not trying to figure out like what kind of funnel do I build and whatever. I want you guys to know exactly what it is. I want you to have perfect certainty so you can go home and execute on the stuff. So that's kind of the game plan. All right. So the first thing that we talked about in the book and also in the webinar, if you had a chance to watch it, is what I call the secret formula. And if you look at my entire business or this entire book, this is kind of sums up what the entire thing is about. And it's funny because when you look at it, it's pretty simple. Like I try to make it as simple as possible. That's really the goal of business. And it took me a long time to kind of figure that out. And so the secret formula, the first step in this is we got to figure out who. Who is the person that you guys want to serve? Who is the person that you feel like you can change their life the most? When I started my business, I never asked that question. I wish I would have. I ran for four, five, six years in a direction trying to sell stuff. I never thought about that concept even once. And because of that, I started attracting customers that I didn't like. They were hard to work with. And it was a very painful process. Some days I'd wake up and I was just sick to my stomach. And I'm sure some of you guys can relate to that. Some of you guys have customers or clients who you wake up and you're like, why in the world do I have to go and help this person today? They're not going to listen to me. They're going to be a nightmare to work with. And I thought that we had, I thought that's, I really thought like if you want to make money, you just got to deal with it. You got to get thick skin and you got to kind of just give into that and work with anybody. And it wasn't until about two years ago for us that I remember waking up and I was so frustrated because I knew I didn't want to go to work. I had coaching calls and things I needed to do and I just, I wasn't, I lost the fire completely. And which is a scary point for me. I'm like, if I ever lose this, you know, that's never a good thing. Like you got to be passionate about what you're doing. And I lost that fire. I remember laying there in bed thinking like, I've got to change something. And about that time, it was actually over Christmas time, Tony Robbins and his wife Sage put out this video and I still remember vividly. He was sitting in Fiji and he got up there and he said, you know, it's a new year. He's like, obviously we can change anytime we want, but the new year gives us this opportunity where we can stand up and like make change. He said, so right now I want you guys to look at yourselves. He says, if you're not happy in your relationship, change it. If you're not happy in your business, change it. You're not happy with whatever it is in your life, with your, with your, with your fitness or your weight loss, whatever it is. Like if you're not happy with something in life right now, I'm giving you permission just to change it. I remember thinking like, you know, I can, I can make that change. I can just change it. I'm just going to do that. And so I said, how do I, how do I create my business in a way where the only people I'm dealing with every day are people that are awesome that I want to hang out with, people that I would literally, if they invited me to go to lunch with them, I'd be excited to go and not like, oh man, I got to go to lunch with these guys, right? And so it started out to this is like, who, like I really had identified who that person was. And I don't think most of us think about it like, who is your dream customer? Who's the person that, that you would hang out with them because you like them, even if you didn't get paid for it. Okay. And if you don't know who that person is, it's hard then to, to be able to get them, right? So we need to know who that person is. I'm going to walk you through a process I went through to really identify who that person was here in a second, but that's the first step. Who is that person? Now, after we know who that person is, then we have the chance to go and find out where those people are at. Okay. And again, if you don't know the who, you can never find the where. And so that's why it's so important to know the who. So now I know the who, now I got to find out where those people at. And the coolest thing about the internet, the reason I love the internet is because it's given a chance for people like me and you who don't have, you know, Walmart or Amazon budgets to be successful because the internet is cool because as you see here in this picture, there's a whole bunch of fish in one spot, right? The internet has this really unique ability to like, categorize people together, right? You think I was thinking about back about when I was a, when I was a wrestler, a Boise state, and one of the, the things that all of us had mandatory study hall every night after practice. And because most of us wrestlers weren't very smart and they didn't want to sell the fill out. And so all of us entire, we go to wrestling practice, eat dinner, and then all of us had to go to the mandatory two hours of study hall every night. And one day I remember sitting there in study hall and I was on the computer and I had one tab with like my homework and the other tab was on the mat.com, which is the biggest wrestling website in the world. And I was in the forums talking and chatting and also having some fun and like commenting on people's things and all these things. And also I looked up and I remember looking around seeing all the other wrestlers in like sitting in the back and looking at every other computer. And I would say probably 90% of the other wrestlers were all on the mat.com at the exact same time. And I was like, how interesting, like all of us are on the exact, we're all congregated on the same spot. And I said, I was thinking like, I bet if you went to every college in America and you looked at the wrestlers and study hall, I bet they're all on the exact same website as well. And, uh, and if you go to the mat.com, you see there's like 50, 60, 70, thousand wrestlers who all congregate there every single day. So if I knew that my who was wrestlers, where are those guys congregating at? Where are they hanging out at? Okay. It's not that hard to find their own one spot. And that happens in every business, every market you guys can think about. I don't care what business you're in right now, your dream clients are congregating together somewhere and you may not know where it is yet, but you've got to find it. Cause when you can find it, then you, there they are, you know, like it's an isolated spot. Now you just got to go and grab those people out and figure out ways to get them out. So that's kind of the next step. Then the third thing there is after you've figured out where these people are at, how do I get those people in? Right? This is like, it's bait. Like what's the bait I'm going to create to get my dream client to come to me. Okay. And so two years ago when we were doing this, I was thinking like, what do I have that that's going to get my dream client to come to me? Okay. And we had, we created a bunch of different products and services and I'll show you, I'll show you my dot com secrets lab book here in a little bit, but we created this book that basically, uh, it was called dot com secrets labs, 108 proven split test winners. And it was just a whole bunch of beautiful books, like 140 pages, all of our different split tests, um, that, uh, that we've been running over the last like four or five years and put this book together. And it is initially I was going to sell for a whole bunch of money, but I decided, you know what, um, I'm going to use this as bait. I was thinking about it like, if you look at the title of the book, like 108 proven split test winners, um, my, my old client, the ones who, their goal was to get rich on the internet. If they saw that they'd be like, I don't know what that means. What is, what's a split test? Why would I even care? And literally my, my offer, my bait would repel the people I don't want to do. Right. I'm not a fisherman, but I'm guessing that if you go in fishing and you know, you want trout versus salmon, there's probably different bait used that, that trout are going to bite and salmon aren't going to. I'm guessing, I don't know. But that's how we're tearing it. So I knew that that book, like the people I don't want to work with are going to be repelled by it. They're just going to bounce off and not even notice it. But my dream clients, people who have a business, who are already making money, who, who, uh, who are doing the, the kind of things I want, the kind of people that I want to sell, uh, 25,000, $100,000 coaching package to and more. When they see that book, it's going to be like, like the most amazing thing in the world. They're going to be so excited. And so I said, you know what, this is the bait that it will attract the person, the who that I want. Um, and so we put that out there and this became bait. And over the last year, um, we've given away over 10,000 copies of that book, which has brought in insane amounts of amazing business owners, people like you guys into, into my world. And so then after I got the bait that pearls people out, then the last thing to figure out, um, one of those mistakes I think most of us make in business is that we're so focused on like, what's the product I sell? Okay. And, um, I don't care what business you're in. If you're in e-commerce or if you're in coaching or if you're in whatever you're in, like the product's not the, the, they're different. So products are not in a business. Okay. If you guys watch Shark Tank, Mark Cuban always says that he's like, you've got a great product. You don't have a business. Okay. For me, like what a business is, is, is not like what can I sell my own client? The end business, the end, the end thing is like, what's the result I can get for him? What, how can I serve these people at the highest level? Okay. And if I, if your thought process is how can I serve these people to highest level, how can I change their lives? How can I blow their minds? And if the goal is the end result, um, that's when the magic starts happening. That's when all the other stuff we're talking about with sales. You build things that get people, um, to want to send up to you to, uh, to get the, the end result you want to, you want to give for them. Okay. And so that's kind of, that's the secret formula. Okay. And if, when you go through the dot com secrets book, you'll notice I'm talking about a lot of say, who is the person you're going after? Where are these people congregating? What's the bait that we're going to create to get those people in? And after you've got them in, what are the funnels we're going to use to send these people up? Okay. And, uh, and that's kind of the, the game plan. It's kind of cool. Last, uh, last week, um, our sales guys signed up, um, somebody we are top level program right now, um, is a million dollar program. So it pays us a hundred thousand dollars down and 10% of their income, uh, until I've been paid a million dollars. So for me, it's like, that's, that's the top of my mountain. That's where I want, like, if I can bring people there, um, that's ideal. Because first off, I can serve these guys at a different level. Like literally, um, they're coming to me. I'm building out everything. I'm writing a copy and build everything for them. And it's really, really cool process. Um, and, uh, and I can give them a result better than anything else. I could do in the world. And in exchange for that, they're paying me a lot of money. I'm only, I'll make a million dollars from this one deal. Um, because my goal is results. What's the result I can get for this person? Okay. And so like I said, you, you, when you go through this process, and we'll talk a lot about this today, the goal is to send people up and you're going to have funnels that, that start down here to give people little results, which will make them want to get bigger results and they'll get them to kind of, kind of send up. And so that's kind of the secret formula. That's, that's the whole concept here with the book, with this training is, is understanding business this way, as opposed to I have people coming to me all the time, Hey, I'm in a network marketing program. I want to sell this. Like, okay, that's, that's, that's like a sliver of it, but you're missing everything else. You're missing who you're trying to serve, where are these people at? How can you get those people? What's the bait you can use to attract them? And then where else do you want to take them? There's so much more you can do than just, than just that one thing. And so that's kind of the secret formula. Now I want to spend one minute on, on the who real quick, because I think this is important enough that, that I want to, I want to emphasize again, I talked about in the book, I talked about in the webinar, but for me, when I, when I was kind of redesigning our business and thinking about who my dream client was, I tried to make it very tangible. I think about who is this person? Like, what do they look like? And so I remember I sat down, I sat down one day and I wanted a, I wanted a, a female version of my dream client and a male version of my dream client. I wanted to kind of see what the both of them look like. And so I sat down and I remember I typed in, I typed in a name. I was like, my dream client, her name's gonna be something like Julie. So I wrote down Julie and I said, now what, what qualities does she have? Okay. Well, I want to work with people that are successful and driven. Like if they're not like already successful, don't want to work with them. Cause because people that are successful in one aspect of life, usually they're six, they can become successful in a lot of other aspects in life. They're not to be successful in business, but they have to be successful in something. Okay. That's the first thing. Second thing is I like working with people that, that feel like their business isn't just like, I have a business, but like they, they're passionate about it. Like they've got a message that they can change the world. Okay. So I wanted to work with people that, that's what they feel. It's not just like I'm trying to make a quick buck, but what's more important to them is, is getting their message out and serving people. Okay. That's kind of person I want. Um, I wanted someone who values her personal growth over money. Um, and I want someone who already has a business that's, that's happening. Okay. I want someone who's already gone through all the beginner stuff and even if they're making a lot of money, but has something kind of up and running. And so that was kind of my criteria. So remember, I went, I went to Google images and I typed in Julie, I typed in successful, I typed in a couple of these keywords there and I click submit. And all of a sudden all these images came up, all these random people. There's some weird looking people. There's like some Walmart type people. And then all of a sudden this picture popped up and I was like, boom, that's, that's her. That's the person I want to work with. And so I grabbed that picture out and that became my customer average. I was Julie. Okay. And I've got this picture in my office and I look at it when I'm trying to figure out like, like when I'm creating products or creating servers or things that I'm like, would Julie want this? Like would this, would this be something that would, that would change her life? If I'm like, no, Julie would not. Like maybe Julie's sister might want it, but she's not going to want it. I'm not going to do it. Uh, one of my friends, Perry Belcher, he, um, uh, I'm probably going to slaughter this story, but it was really cool when he told it to me. Um, he used to work at a, at a company. Um, he actually invented, uh, he's ever used that palm rail, um, liquid hand stuff. So he invented that he was trying to make these candles, holding screwed up. They kept burning up and catching on fire. And, uh, they were going to throw this huge batch of stuff away basically. And, and some guys like, well, the cool thing about it is you can rent your hands and the, the alcohol kills the germs. And he was like, let's package this thing up. So he packaged up, sold a whole bunch of them, ended up selling the company, uh, to some guys who ended up selling it to a, to a, it was kind of cool story. But he told me that when he was doing that product and some of his other products that, uh, he would go into companies like bed, bath and beyond and things like that. And he would take these products and try to pitch it to him. And he was telling me this story. He said that, uh, I think it was bed, bath and beyond. He was there and he was talking to like the executives and trying to pitch. Like, here's the product. Here's why it's awesome. And, uh, and the people kept saying, well, I don't, I don't know if, uh, I can't remember the name. I don't know, let's say Julie. I don't know if, uh, I don't know if Julie would like that. And he's like, okay. And then it shows something else. I don't think Julie liked that. And finally he's like, you know what, if Julie's a decision maker, can we bring her in here? Cause I like to talk to Julie directly. And they're like, oh no, Julie's not a real person. Julie's our, our dream client. And we base all of our decisions off with Julie, want this or not. And, uh, he was like, oh, it had that like aha moment. And I was like, how cool is that the big huge corporations that are making billions and billions of dollars, they do the same process as well. And most of us aren't. Okay. So there was Julie. I was our first one. Next one. I wanted the same thing for, for a man. Um, and if it's, it's funny, if you look at my coaching program right now, um, uh, it's kind of funny, like with, uh, with Julie, um, we signed up a lady the other day named Julie and then she went through the training and, uh, she messaged me. She said, Rosal. She's like, my name's Julie. Um, she's like, I'm successful here. She's like, anyway, she's like, you nailed it to a T. She's like, I am Julie. I was like, and literally she was named Julie. It was like so funny. I'm standing with Mike, like, uh, we just got a guy named Mike in our inner circle who looks almost identical to Mike. It's crazy. Um, anyway, it's the same thing. Like when I was developing Mike, I said, I wanted a former athlete because I love working with athletes. Um, I want someone who's changed, who's already had a chance to change and affect someone's life in a little way. They've helped a friend or a client or someone and they, they're so passionate about the effect and the result they got for some, they want to blow it up and take over the world. Um, I wanted to get the same thing. Some of the values, growth over money and already has a business kind of running. And so we developed that went to Google images and boom, Mike popped up. I said, that's my guy. And I grabbed the picture and now I've got, I've got, now I know who my who is. I know exactly who those people are. Now that I know who they are, it was very tangible and I can figure out where to find them and everything else we're trying to, you know, where to find them. What kind of bait is going to get them and what kind of results I can get for them. So I want to make sure you guys think about that a little bit and, uh, they're kind of focusing on it. Okay. So that's the first thing. Now, um, if you guys go to your, uh, to your blueprint here, the first step here, I have is, is result. And I want to talk about this for a little bit because, um, I don't think, in fact, this is the first situation I've ever talked about this ever. Um, it wasn't in the book. It wasn't in anything else because, um, I never thought about it this way before. And over the last two or three months, there's been this trend of people who come into our coaching programs and things like that, where they come in and, uh, they have an idea or they've got something they want to do, but they've never, they've never actually gotten a result for someone before and they want to start selling this, this concept, right? They read a book or they went to a seminar and also they want to start teaching, you know, they're, they're fired up and they're passionate. They want to change the world, but they haven't ever gotten a result for something before. And, um, and, uh, it's been interesting. I think over the last month, I would say probably five or six people, um, when they come in our program, I tell them, this is the first step you got to do. And I wanted to kind of bring this back to you guys. Cause I think it's, it's the key. So what you need to do is if, and I'm curious, how many of you guys in your business you're in right now, do you have tangible results that you've gotten for somebody? Okay. About half of you guys and half of you guys haven't. So, um, so I want to tell you a story. This is a guy named Drew Canole. And, uh, uh, my wife said he's pretty much the best looking man on earth. Um, I don't know about that, but, uh, anyway, so Drew is a really, really cool guy. He owns a company called FitLife TV. Um, they have like 1.5 million Facebook followers and a whole bunch of stuff. And, uh, and, uh, big into juicing and all that kind of stuff. And so I had a chance to get to know him. I went to his website, I thought it was cool. I had a mutual friend, had him connect, introduced me to him. And, um, I was in San Diego one day and so, uh, it was kind of, it's kind of cool. Like, uh, I called him up. I was like, Hey, you're really knowing me, but you know, Carl taught, you know, blah, blah, blah, introduced me and I love to come meet you. And he was like, all right. He said, that's cool. Here's my address. Come, come meet me. So I jumped in a, I jumped in an Uber, drove up to his, uh, his place and I, and I showed up there. I started talking about, Hey man, just, um, seen your stuff online. Thought you're really cool. I was in San Diego. I wanted to meet you. And it looks at me and he says, it was so cool. He said, um, uh, he said, this is the weirdest thing. He said, three weeks ago, I bought dot com secrets labs off Facebook. He's like, I was reading, I remember thinking about how cool would it be to have Russell come and do all the stuff for our business. He said, and right now you're sitting in my living room. I don't know how you got here. You called me today. Now you're sitting in my living room. He's like, this is the weirdest thing in the world. Okay. And I was thinking that I like drew is like my customer out, like my dream client. Right. And, uh, and I had a couple of options that I could have been like, well, drew, you know, you should give me a hundred thousand dollars and I'll help. Blah, blah, blah. But I was like, you know what? Like, uh, I don't, I don't want to, I don't want to do it. He's got a bunch of stuff happening. It's really, really successful. And anyway, we kind of just talked about business a little bit. And then, um, my family was in San Diego. So I spent a couple of hours and then jumped in my family went to Legoland and didn't see me again for a couple of months. And then a few months later, I was just sitting there in bed trying to think like, how can I, how can I get more of my dream clients and how can I, how can I get more of the kind of people I want to be working with? And I had this high to pop my head. I said, you know what? Like drew is like my dream client. Someone who's got a really successful business, a bunch of stuff happening. Uh, they're having some struggles and can't quite get through the next level. And I said, I have an idea. So I called him up and said, Hey, Drew, this is the deal. Um, I would love to come out to your office. Um, typically I charge people $25,000. I'll come in, I'll spend a whole day with you guys and I'm not going to charge you anything just if, but if, but if I, if I come, I'd love if I could videotape it and if it helps you guys, love for you guys and give me a video afterwards and he was like, yeah, that'd be awesome. And so, uh, jumped into the plan, paid my own way, flew out to, flew out San Diego, got there in a conference room, kind of like this. His whole team was there and, uh, they spent about an hour giving me everything in their business, like the ups and the downs and all sorts of stuff. And, uh, and they were in a spot. I didn't realize this at the time. They were in a spot where, um, they were making over a million dollars a year, but they were losing money every single month. Like they were not profitable. They had been, but because Facebook had made some changes and things like that, they were, they were struggling. And so, uh, we looked at everything and I sat down and we built out, um, uh, kind of a model and a blueprint what to do. Um, it was funny because they didn't have whiteboard for me. So I had markers and we were drawn all over the windows in the, in the place and had all this stuff and, uh, and literally came and just tried to serve him the best way that I could. And as much as I could, and, um, what was cool is that after, after we came and did the stuff form, um, about a month later, they rolled out the new funnels and new stuff and they went and just blew it up. They went from, um, from being unprofitable to where they were, uh, they were, they were net in like 20 to 30 grand a day consistently from day one. Boom, boom, boom. Just like, just amazing, amazing, like turnaround, which was really, really cool. And, uh, and we've, they let us videotape testimonials of him and his staff and all sorts of stuff. And it was awesome. And then I came back and I take those, took those videos, put them online. And we started driving Facebook ads to these videos of him talking about Russell came and just blew our minds, changed our world and guess what happened. Also, all these people started seeing, it wasn't me saying, Hey, I'm really good at being a business coach. You guys should come hire me. They started seeing Drew talk about, man, Russell changed my life. Boom, all these things. And he started sharing his results. We drove traffic to that and overnight thousands of like his face, like produces applications for our coaching program. Like crazy. Like tons of people started coming in because we, we gave him this result. Okay. And so for you guys, what I want you to start thinking about, and we're gonna have, we're gonna break for a second for you guys to do this is, is, uh, I want you to think about, um, with whatever your business is or your product, your service, um, do you have someone that you, you, you, you, you, you have changed their life? Have you given them the result? You've taken them through this process at your highest level. And if not, I need you guys to work for free for someone. I need you to find one or two or three people because this is going to fuel and drive everything out to you. The better success stories you have, more you've changed someone's life, the better, uh, the more people you're going to attract because of that. Okay. So the first step is start thinking like, like, of the people I have right now, who are the ones that I can, that I can serve the most. Um, or if I don't have one, like who's someone I can volunteer my time, who can I go work for for free? Okay. Um, there's a kid that, that lives, uh, that goes to church with us. And, uh, he was like 16 or 17 years old and he was, uh, trying to get a job last summer and he went and applied like 20 different places and every place is like, sorry, not hiring, not hiring. And, uh, and finally got so frustrated that he said, you know what? And he went down to the mall and he walked into some, I don't know, I think it was the buckle or something like that and walked in and, uh, and just said, Hey, I want to see if I can work for free for you guys. And they're like, what are you talking about? He's like, I've got nothing to do. Do you mind if I just fold clothes for you for a while? They're like, all right. So you start folding clothes and everything is just folding clothes. Like crazy and did that one day. Next day came back, started folding clothes and after two or three days working for free, these guys came and said, Hey, do you mind if we, uh, if we give you a job, he's like, yeah, it'd be awesome. And boom, he got a job. Like his dream job. Right. And, uh, I think that's the key guys is like, if you guys want to like slingshot your success, you've got to have some just amazing successors. You've got to change people's lives. And, uh, if that means that initially you've got to go and volunteer time, it's worth it. I promise you it's worth it. So there's Drew. This is a picture of, of, uh, Liz. And the Liz is someone I didn't do it for free. She signed it for our $25,000 program, but we're saying to him gave her a result. And, um, the other thing is a lot of times you've had results for people and you can say, yeah, I helped so and so lose weight. I had, you had so and so do this, this, this, but the problem is like you knowing that you have a result does not turn into money for you. Okay. So the second step is after you've gotten these results, you have to capture them in a way that's going to inspire people. Okay. So I want to tell you something. Um, hiring Russell Brunson was bar none, one of the best decisions I've ever made in my entire life. And I want to tell you that when you work with him, he takes you on this journey that you, you, you won't even know that, um, that you can go on. Like it's like I, at the very beginning, I had all these hopes and dreams and visions and I honestly thought that yay, I'm working with the overnight success maker and like, I thought, whoa, I'm going to make like $500,000 in the first month of working with him. But you know what the eventuality is that sometimes in life, it doesn't always work out the exact way that you want it to work out. And that's certainly where it got to for me. So my 2014 was one of the hardest years I've ever had in my entire life. Like there was cancer in the family. Um, people were in hospital. I thought I was going to, um, I, at one stage, I, um, you know, I thought I was going to lose my partner. Like it just wasn't a pleasant scenario at all. And by losing my partner, I mean like hospitalization, like really not like from really bad health issues. And you know, there were just times when I promised you I wanted to give up. And sometimes I actually, when I, when I say that, I don't just mean give up in business, like I, I, for me, when I, like when I hire Russell, it really was like do, like sounds really dramatic, but like my life's mission, it's so, it's so big, like I really, really, really wanted to connect with someone who could get me to the next level, who could help me really get from that really stranded, grounded place, you know, sitting at my office every single day to a place where I'm, you know, helping like people all around the world. And that's why I chose Russell. Um, but man, it was hard. It was really, really, really hard at times to have to deal with those things that come up in your personal life all the time. Like it was not an easy scenario in 2014, but I'm really, really, really grateful that I listened to Russell. I'm really grateful that I hired him as a coach. Like the man has changed my life forever. Like, you know, once you, like my mind has been blown completely. And there's no way back. Like I know that anything is possible now. So the, the reason why I showed it was not to kind of brag about anything. The reason is because I promised you guys that right now, most of you guys have stories like that of people's lives you've changed. And can I see him? Like where are they at? You've got to, you've got to capture because that's the goal. Having it, giving someone the result and blowing their minds one thing, but you've got to have some kind of proof elements so you can, you can then show people. Now, I was talking to Mike about this yesterday and he's like, well, that's cool, but I don't have a, I can't hire a huge film crew, all these kinds of things. Ideally, yeah, you want to, you know, make them as good as possible. But I sat down with him yesterday and I said, watch, I can do one of these in, in about 30 seconds and won't be quite as good as that, but it's going to be really, really close. It's funny because I've seen people who do have testimonial and testimonial videos of their clients. And this is what they're typically like. Persons awkwardly standing here. Sometimes they got a little microphone. It looks horrible. Usually it's really blown up screen, like really small in the middle. And the person's like, so I went to Keith's class and it was really awesome. And I learned how to tap people out and I'm definitely coming back again next week. And that's how like most people's testimonials are. It doesn't inspire anything, right? So I'm going to show you guys really quick in about 30 seconds, how to make a video on the low budget to make it super easy and super powerful. So a couple of things that are important. So I did this with Mike last night, just with my iPhone. So you can, the iPhones and iPads, a couple of things that are key. First off is I think the most powerful ways you've got to have some kind of interesting background. Like it shouldn't be against a white wall or something born. I go outside, find something. This one that went outside and had kind of this nice trees and stuff like that. I like having people off centered. For some reason it seems more sincere. It seems really, really cool. Second thing is that the person that's being the testimonial should not be looking at the camera. Okay. If I'm filming this and looking at the camera, when there's a testimony where someone's looking at the camera, it seems very insincere. I don't know why, but it just does. And so if I'm filming, like when I filmed Mike yesterday, I had the camera, had my phone right here and I said, Mike, look right here. And I had my eyes right next to the camera. And so he's looking, he's looking away. Do you know what Liz was looking at the camera? She was looking away like she was being interviewed by someone. So you hold the camera, have them look, look to the side of the camera, not at the camera. That's the next step. And then second, you've got to be a reporter. You've got to feed them the questions that you want. Okay. Um, the first time I learned this, we hired this guy named Jonathan Otto who does a whole bunch of really good videos and, uh, and we hired him for a week. He came out here for a week. He was doing stuff. And I found that like he wasn't amazing at capturing video, but he was amazing at asking questions. And I looked at the process he did and that's what, that's what made these videos magic. And so for you, instead of like, give me some microphones, like give me testimonial, right? Okay. So it was really cool. And I think I liked it. You've got to, you've got to be the one, like the Oprah Winfrey, right? Where you're bringing this out. And so what I do typically is I start asking questions. I say, okay, uh, before you got started with me, tell me what your life was like before you got started and we're taking someone on a journey. They want them to tell me, okay, you know, Liz talked about where she was at, talk about the journey she went through and then where she's at right now. Okay. Beginning journey and then the results. So you ask them say, um, you get them looking my eye right here when we talk. Okay. So when you got started with me, what was, uh, what was your life like before we got started and they'll say, Hey, this is what happened. They'll start talking back to you directly. Okay. And notice how much more sincere that sounds. They used to second, the second question, you know, second or third, like, now when you're going to the process, what were the struggles you went through? Like what were the hurdles you had? And they can act. Well, you know, the biggest hurdles I had was I got stuck here. I got stuck here. Okay. That's awesome. Now, what was the reason why you got started with me? I got to start with you because of this and because of this. And these are the reasons why I thought I'd be, I would work with you. Can I, was it easy when you first got started? What, what was the process when you first got started? I first got started. It was, it was scary. It was hard. It was difficult. Um, I didn't know what to do, but, but I decided to take that leap of faith and do it. Okay. Cool. So after you did that and you started getting results like, what's your life like now? Oh, my life's amazing. I get to change me from this to this and it's been the greatest thing in the world. Boom. Okay. That's a testimony on how that's magic, right? It's me feeding a couple of questions to get their journey. Where did they begin at? What's the process they went through and what's the result at the end? Okay. Now afterwards you gotta do some post production. I don't know how to edit stuff. I've got brand new edits videos for me and a couple of other people. Um, I've heard and my little kids do this. So I know it's possible on their iPad. They like movie maker, I think. And the key is like, you don't want to have you asking the question. Okay. And so one thing you coach this person through is that when you, I asked you like, what was the beginning of your journey? They can't say Detroit. Like they have to kind of like restate your question. Right. So say, uh, well, the beginning of the journey for me was whatever. So I get them to restate the question because next thing is you're going to cut out your, you asking the questions. You don't want your voice in there. You want them telling the story and last step out of music track and boom, you've got gold that fast. Okay. So that's the way you guys capture these. By doing this, but who, who am I actually earning from? And the way you built the avatar, you know, your perfect client, it, uh, actually after rewatching the webinar and practicing that, just like literally visualizing who is this person, you know, and what is it, what is their life like? You know, almost like writing a screenplay. What's the backstory to this person? You know, who's their, who's their spouse? Who are their kids? What are they dealing with on a daily basis? And, and just really creating that bond with that person so that I'm not just, Hey, I got a video for you. I'm actually showing up for them. I'm showing up for them in a relationship that, you know, really honors who they are so that the intention of our company is to be a tremendous benefit to, to these people. And so to me, again, I think that that's the biggest takeaway from the first part is we're dealing with human beings who have lives just like us and they need support. And so rather than just how much can I get from this, you know, how much can I serve these folks? Understanding who our customer is exactly is wonderful. Understanding how to do a good testimonial. I think that's great. One of the things that I really took away was stop focusing so much on the product and build the business around it. There's products everywhere and everybody has a product, but why are those products failing? Because they don't have a business built around it or a way to get it out to the market. So to me, it's to stop focusing so much on the product and what is my business? How do I get my business wrapped around the product? So that it's out to everybody. I think doing testimonials, understanding and defining who my customer is and making sure that I know my why. This, this, uh, this image here is in, is in your book. That a cool little sketch or what? So this kind of goes against some of the, the initial mindset stuff as well. If you look at how a lot of people get into this or any business, usually you come and you've got a product, right? You like, oh, here's a product, right? I joined some company and here's my opportunity or you, you come with something and you try to start a building a business around there. I did the same thing. That's why I started it. I made some product came out to try to sell it and I had some initial success, but it wasn't, it never really blew up for me until I understood this principle right here. Okay. The very first question you got to ask yourself is who, okay. Who is it that you actually want to serve? You know, for probably seven or eight years of my business, I didn't know who that person was. I was just selling stuff and selling stuff. Okay. Um, it wasn't until I sat back and, and really said like, who don't want to sell to like who's the kind of person I'm going to be working with every single day. Okay. Cause for most of you, this is, this is going to become a full time. If it's not right now, it'll become a full time gig, right? This is your thing. So you have to like be in love with the person you want to serve. Okay. And so for us and our company, we sat down one day about a year ago and I was like, you know, right now we're selling a lot of people were selling brand new beginners, they nothing was happening. I was like, I want to work with, I want to work with people who are, who are, who are having success and we can help take them to that next level. And so we sat down and we said, let's, let's figure out who that person is. So literally this is the process we went through. You guess she was that same thing. I sat down, I wrote, I wrote out a girl's name in my mind. I said, I want to work with someone named Julie. Like she's like my dream person. Okay. I said, now what are the characteristics of Julie? I'm like, well, she's successful. She's a driven woman. She wants success. We're literally bad. Also Julie, she's got a message and she has this message and she knows that she could change the world. She could just get it out to more people, but she has no idea how to do that. Maybe she's doing it a little bit, but just not to the level she wants to yet. Okay. She values her own personal growth over the money she's going to make. That's kind of people I want to work with. And then the last thing is that she's already got a business is making some money. Okay. I want someone who's moving forward and doing some stuff. So I wrote all those characteristics. I went to Google, Google images. I typed in Julie, successful driven business woman. Um, and I typed in some of these keywords here and clicks, I clicked the button and Google images, all these faces are showing up. Right. I'm looking through my note. Don't want to work there. Don't work there. All of a sudden I saw Julie. I was like, boom, that's what I want to work with. Okay. And I'm curious in this room, the women in this room, who here relates to Julie? Who here does that kind of sound like you a little bit? Okay. There's a reason for that. Okay. We architected to get the right kind of person in the room. Who wants to see my male, my male avatar. Boom. Here's Mike. So Mike is a former athlete. How many former athletes we have in the room right now? Isn't that weird? How when you know who you want, you can get them. Okay. You value growth over money and he's already got something happening and he's just wants to take the next level. Okay. Does that speak to a lot of you guys? That interesting? Okay. None of you guys would have came to my old events. I promise you, because I wasn't targeting the right kind of person. Wasn't until we were very, very clear on exactly who we wanted to get you guys in the room. The interesting, interesting to me. Um, you can ask Brent, ask anybody who's been on my team for more than five or six years. Um, it, the way we got the caliber of P and I, this speaks a lot to you guys. You guys are a high caliber person. The way we got you guys in the room was through this process. Okay. So that's the first thing is who, who is that you want to talk to? Okay. And for all you guys, you got to figure out who that person is. I highly recommend doing the Google image thing. Find that person, print it out, put them on a poster, have them on your wall. Cause you want to see that person every single day. I was talking to Perry Belcher about this and he said that when he used to sell stuff, retail, it's like bed, bathroom, beyond and stuff. He, uh, like he, I don't know if you guys know Perry's whole story, but, um, he ever used that, that, um, uh, Palmarelle hand sanitizer stuff. You know, I'm talking about the liquid stuff. Perry invented that. He was trying to make these flammable or these, these wax candles and it didn't work and they kept catching on fire. And then the guy was like, well, you know, there's, there's the sanitizer hands. He's like, are you serious? Package and a ball starts selling it made millions of dollars sold to a guy for one point something million dollars. And that guy turned around the next like a week later and sold it to, uh, to, um, uh, Purell for like $50 million or some crazy now. It's not like that. But anyway, um, I was talking to Perry, he said, when I used to go pitch stuff to, to, um, to retail place, like bed, bathroom, beyond. He said, I would go talk to the person and I'd like, this is the product. And he said that the buyers would, said that the buyers would, uh, would look to them like, I don't know if Julie's going to like this. He's like, Julie, like who, let me talk to, if she's the decision maker, I want to talk to Julie. And then I know, Julie's our ideal customer. We, we make all of our decisions based on if Julie's going to want to buy it or not. Interesting. That's really cool. The big companies are doing that. We should be doing that as well. So I highly recommend figuring out exactly who it is you want because you're going to be hanging out with these people. Okay. I spent four days of my life hanging, like working, getting you guys here to hang out with you. It's not like you guys I want to, you're the kind of people I want to hang out with. Okay. If you don't design that from the very beginning, you're going to get a lot of people you do not like. Okay. Not that I didn't like my old customers, but I didn't like my old customers. Okay. Um, that's the first thing. The second step is where are these people at? Okay. Everyone's like, I talk, how do you get traffic? How do you get traffic? Like the traffic, it's a two step process and we're going to go over a lot of that today, but the first step is who are people at and then where are they at? Okay. Until you know exactly where they're at, you can't get traffic. You're like, I want to do Facebook. Like whether you're people on Facebook, I don't know. I just Facebook, I saw some ad on, I think Facebook. No, you got to know exactly where those people are at. If you're going to target them. Okay. The next thing is you got to have the right bait to get the person in the door. Okay. How many of you guys ended up somehow here at this event because you saw my book, 108 split tests. Okay. I would bet you more than half of you guys. If you will, if we look back at ourselves funnel, um, when we started kind of rearchitecting our business about a year ago, I looked at all of our old offers that we were putting out there and the offers we were putting out there, the bait we were throwing out into the water was all like, get rich quick on the internet, make money now. Okay. What kind of people does that target? If I throw that bait in the water, that's the kind of person I'm going to get. When we shifted the bait to something that attracted the ideal person, it changed our audience, changed everything about our company. So, and we're going to talk a lot about how to create your offers and stuff like that. But you got to look at what's the bait you're using because that is, if you use the wrong bait, you're not going to get the person you want. Use the right bait, you'll get the exact person you want into the room. Okay. Or into, into the, the, the place that you want to sell them. Okay. And then the last step here is now you've got the, this person, where do you want to take him to? And we're talking a lot about this in the next session, but, but where do you want to take this person to? Okay. I don't look at business anymore as like, here's a product I'm selling. It's where do I want to take this person to? Okay. My dream for all of you guys here is to be in our, the group we did yesterday. Okay. The group we did yesterday, everyone paid $25,000 to be there. My goal is for you guys to get to, to those who aren't in that group right now to get you to a spot where you can afford that so you can come in that group. Cause that's where I can serve you at the highest level. So you guys were in the meeting yesterday. Was it awesome? Yeah. And so I want to be able to serve you guys at that level. That's what I want to take you all at. And you're not, some of you guys aren't there yet. Some of you guys will be there soon. I do want to make tons more money, well I do want to make tons more money. But the real reason is because I want to serve you guys at a different level. And I can't do that in bigger groups, the smaller groups I can do when you charge more and I can, I can serve at a different level. And that's why we have hiring things. Same thing for you is where, where do you want to take people? What's your end goal with them? And if you think about that, it's going to help everything else we're doing. All right.
